name: Grass Cutting Sword
description: The Grass Cutting Sword, also known as Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi, is one of the Three Sacred Treasures of Japan, representing valor. Legend states that the sword was discovered within the body of the eight-headed serpent Yamata no Orochi, which was slain by the storm god Susanoo. Originally named Ame-no-Murakumo-no-Tsurugi ('Sword of the Gathering Clouds of Heaven'), it gained its more common name after Susanoo used it to cut through grass and discovered it could control the wind. The sword is said to possess immense power, capable of cleaving through any obstacle and bestowing victory upon its wielder. It symbolizes the emperor's authority and power, and its presence is crucial for the legitimacy of the imperial throne. The sword is believed to be housed at the Atsuta Shrine in Nagoya, although it is never publicly displayed, adding to its mystique and legendary status. The exact appearance of the sword remains unknown, shrouded in secrecy and reverence. Its legacy continues to inspire awe and respect, playing a significant role in Japanese culture and national identity.
subtitle: Legendary blade of valor and power
origin: Discovered inside the Yamata no Orochi serpent

keyMyths.title: Susanoo and the Yamata no Orochi
keyMyths.description: Susanoo slays the eight-headed serpent and discovers the sword within its body, initially naming it Ame-no-Murakumo-no-Tsurugi.

keyMyths.title: Yamato Takeru's Escape
keyMyths.description: Yamato Takeru uses the sword to cut through tall grass set ablaze by his enemies, renaming it Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi.

sources.author: Anonymous
sources.description: Records of Ancient Matters, a foundational text of Japanese mythology.
sources.text: Records of Ancient Matters, a foundational text of Japanese mythology.
